titleStmt.title: The Political Adviser for Germany ( Murphy ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Political Adviser for Germany ( Murphy ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 3006. As anticipated, 69th meeting Control CouncilI.e., Allied Control Council for Germany; for additional documentation on the participation of the United States in the work of the Allied Control Authority for Germany, see pp. 831 ff. held August 30 brought forth lengthy Soviet protest against new British-American bizonal industry level announcement. Referring to governmental note August 18,Regarding the note of August 18, 1947, from Soviet Chargé Tsarapkin to the Acting Secretary of State, see the editorial note, p. 1039. latest bizonal measures branded as rupture of Potsdam Agreement and quadripartite procedure, and another step in process of dismemberment of Germany, following original bizonal merger and subsequent establishment Economic Council. German resources will thus be taken away from German hands and come under influence certain foreign monopoly interests.

French memberGeneral Koenig. referred only to official French communiqué published day before. British memberMarshal Douglas. quoted in his reply from official Foreign Office reply to Soviet Government. US member,General Clay. while reserving right reply later in detail, emphasized great efforts made by US delegation for 2 years to achieve economic unification Germany, that invitation to other powers to join present bi-zonal merger still open, but meanwhile US not prepared sit by and watch American zone become economic quagmire unable support self or contribute European recovery.
